# Research cold-email drafts for Celina Lin

Review the employer posting and lab website before sending. Replace generic recipients with a specific PI or hiring manager when possible.

## lululemon — Educator / The Grove at Shrewsbury

**Subject:** Interest in Educator / The Grove at Shrewsbury — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear lululemon Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Educator / The Grove at Shrewsbury opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a54458be726ec56126a9a74

---

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Research & Development Rotational Program Associate opportunity with Pfizer at Pfizer.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

## Aptiv — Employment Trainer-PT

**Subject:** Interest in Employment Trainer-PT — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Aptiv Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Employment Trainer-PT opportunity with Aptiv at Aptiv.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a54de0271848b0020433a3b

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Educator / Music Lane opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a544595e726ec56126a9a7a

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Part-time Educator / Scottsdale Fashion Square opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a5444198ef95364ead94f50

**Subject:** Interest in Educator / Thruway/Winston-Salem (3 Months-July-Sept) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear lululemon Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Educator / Thruway/Winston-Salem (3 Months-July-Sept) opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a5444028ef95364ead94f37

**Subject:** Interest in Part-Time Educator (Permanent) / Jackson Hole — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear lululemon Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Part-Time Educator (Permanent) / Jackson Hole opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. **Subject:** Interest in Part-Time Educator / West County Center — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear lululemon Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Part-Time Educator / West County Center opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a54419a8a74e077472fb280

## lululemon — Educator / Lululemon - Cow Hollow, San Francisco, CA

**Subject:** Interest in Educator / Lululemon - Cow Hollow, San Francisco, CA — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear lululemon Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Educator / Lululemon - Cow Hollow, San Francisco, CA opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a544171d007ee02d95fe2b6

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Full Time Educator / Cherry Hill Mall opportunity with lululemon at lululemon.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

## PSC by Rocket Lab — Materials and Process Engineer I

**Subject:** Interest in Materials and Process Engineer I — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear PSC by Rocket Lab Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Materials and Process Engineer I opportunity with PSC by Rocket Lab at PSC by Rocket Lab.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Daycare Teachers-We're Growing! opportunity with Little Sprouts, LLC at Little Sprouts, LLC.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## Boys & Girls Clubs of Topeka — Youth Development Leader

**Subject:** Interest in Youth Development Leader — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Boys & Girls Clubs of Topeka Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Youth Development Leader opportunity with Boys & Girls Clubs of Topeka at Boys & Girls Clubs of Topeka.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## Kaplan — Student Brand Ambassador Ohio State University (Main Campus)

**Subject:** Interest in Student Brand Ambassador Ohio State University (Main Campus) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Kaplan Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Student Brand Ambassador Ohio State University (Main Campus) opportunity with Kaplan at Kaplan.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a462bbf0dd56c76cc2f708e

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Dear Celerion Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Laboratory Operations Assistant - Part-Time opportunity with Celerion at Celerion.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a52d9e18ef95364ead90167

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I am reaching out about the 2026 Fall (Sep) / Sessional Instructional Asst / RSM5604H - Leadership Development Practicum Job Details / University of Toronto opportunity with University of Toronto - Woodsworth College at University of Toronto - Woodsworth College.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## Sullivan & Cromwell LLP — Assistant - Learning & Development

**Subject:** Interest in Assistant - Learning & Development — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Sullivan & Cromwell LLP Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Assistant - Learning & Development opportunity with Sullivan & Cromwell LLP at Sullivan & Cromwell LLP.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## SchoolSpring — Henley Elementary School Paraprofessional – Special Education/ Restrictive Structured and Intensive -Student Behavior / Success

**Subject:** Interest in Henley Elementary School Paraprofessional – Special Education/ Restrictive Structured and Intensive -Student Behavior / Success — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Henley Elementary School Paraprofessional – Special Education/ Restrictive Structured and Intensive -Student Behavior / Success opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## SchoolSpring — Paraeducator - Early Childhood Center

**Subject:** Interest in Paraeducator - Early Childhood Center — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Paraeducator - Early Childhood Center opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

**Subject:** Interest in Clinical Research Coordinator - Tumor Institute — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Providence Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Clinical Research Coordinator - Tumor Institute opportunity with Providence at Providence.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a365f6329c90c607e4e4959

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Administrative Assistant opportunity with New York Institute of Technology at New York Institute of Technology.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

**Subject:** Interest in Golf, Tennis, and Sports Content Creators in Austin, TX / TeachMe.To — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear TeachMe.To Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Golf, Tennis, and Sports Content Creators in Austin, TX / TeachMe.To opportunity with TeachMe.To at TeachMe.To.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## SchoolSpring — Tutor - AVID - Part-Time - Kino Junior High School

**Subject:** Interest in Tutor - AVID - Part-Time - Kino Junior High School — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Tutor - AVID - Part-Time - Kino Junior High School opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Dear Weber State University Department of Automotive Technology Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the After School Program Coordinator opportunity with Weber State University Department of Automotive Technology at Weber State University Department of Automotive Technology.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a4e9ff515b4965afd58410d

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Extended Day Assistant Teacher (SY26-27) opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

I am reaching out about the Graduate Assistant for Institutional Research, Effectiveness, and Data Insights opportunity with Saginaw Valley State University at Saginaw Valley State University.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

## May Institute — ASSISTANT PROGRAM COORDINATOR

**Subject:** Interest in ASSISTANT PROGRAM COORDINATOR — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear May Institute Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the ASSISTANT PROGRAM COORDINATOR opportunity with May Institute at May Institute.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Instructional Assistant II - (Special Education - Cravens) - 2026/2027 school year opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

**Subject:** Interest in Bulletin 4635 Building Substitute at Ross Elementary School for 2026-27 School Year — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Bulletin 4635 Building Substitute at Ross Elementary School for 2026-27 School Year opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## Montgomery County Public Schools — FY27: School Secretary I, 8 hrs / 10 mos, Seven Locks ES, Ad closes 7/16/26

**Subject:** Interest in FY27: School Secretary I, 8 hrs / 10 mos, Seven Locks ES, Ad closes 7/16/26 — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Montgomery County Public Schools Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the FY27: School Secretary I, 8 hrs / 10 mos, Seven Locks ES, Ad closes 7/16/26 opportunity with Montgomery County Public Schools at Montgomery County Public Schools.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Teacher Assistant (Manhattan Site) opportunity with GPM Investments, LLC at GPM Investments, LLC.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Administrative Support-Student Assistant (Basic Needs Lancaster Center) opportunity with Dallas College at Dallas College.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Marketing Content Creator/Producer opportunity with University of Wisconsin-Madison School of Pharmacy at University of Wisconsin-Madison School of Pharmacy.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Behavior Analyst in Training - Standley Lake opportunity with Soar Autism Center at Soar Autism Center.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Daycare Closing Teacher *Needed until 6pm* opportunity with Little Sprouts, LLC at Little Sprouts, LLC.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the High-Dosage Tutoring Paraprofessional - 6.5 hrs/day -Mills Elementary School opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Machine Learning Engineer opportunity with Genentech / Roche at Genentech / Roche.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the IT Operations Manager opportunity with Arc Institute at Arc Institute.

I am especially interested in the group's effort to integrate multi-omic and clinical data with AI for biomarker and therapeutic discovery. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in bioinformatics, pipeline development, and an LLM-assisted competency-mapping project has made me especially interested in rigorous, interpretable AI for biomedical data.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## Natera — Clinical Field Specialist

**Subject:** Interest in Clinical Field Specialist — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Clinical Field Specialist opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

## Modal — Research and Education Partnerships Manager

**Subject:** Interest in Research and Education Partnerships Manager — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Modal Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Research and Education Partnerships Manager opportunity with Modal at Modal.

I am especially interested in building scalable data systems that let research and clinical teams use biological data reliably. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## Bristol Myers Squibb — Bioprocess Associate - Single Use Facility

**Subject:** Interest in Bioprocess Associate - Single Use Facility — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Bristol Myers Squibb Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Bioprocess Associate - Single Use Facility opportunity with Bristol Myers Squibb at Bristol Myers Squibb.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://bristolmyerssquibb.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/BMS/job/Devens---MA---US/Bioprocess-Associate---Single-Use-Facility_R1603355

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Sales Development Representative opportunity with Ramp at Ramp.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/ramp/d2d32a6d-5f29-4708-83d0-03b4e44c031d

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.
